strategies.name: SEP-IRA / Solo 401(k) for self-employed filer
strategies.trigger: netSeIncome >= 30000 AND no existing SEP/Solo401k retirement adjustment AND filingStatus != married_filing_separately
strategies.formula: contribution = min((netSeIncome - halfSeDeduction) * 0.20, planLimit); estSavings = contribution * (federalMarginalRate + stateMarginalRate)
prerequisiteData: Whether the client has any other retirement plan (SIMPLE, 401(k))
Whether the client wants employee-deferral capacity (Solo 401(k)) vs employer-only (SEP)
strategies.action: Open and fund a SEP-IRA (or Solo 401(k) if employee-deferral desired) by the extended return deadline. Contribution = ~{{contribution}}; estimated combined-rate savings ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Pass-Through Entity Tax (PTET) election to bypass SALT cap
strategies.trigger: K-1 pass-through income > 0 AND resident state has PTET regime AND SALT cap binds (saltDeductible == $10,000)
prerequisiteData: Confirm entity is eligible for PTET in its state (typically S-corp or partnership)
Confirm all owners consent to PTET election where state requires unanimous consent
Estimated state tax payable from the entity
strategies.action: Have the pass-through entity make a PTET election for the next tax year so state income tax is paid + deducted at the entity level, bypassing the $10k federal SALT cap. Estimated federal benefit ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Bunch itemized deductions in alternate years
strategies.trigger: itemizedTotal between 85% and 115% of stdDed AND charitableCash > 0
strategies.formula: estSavings = stdDed * 0.5 * marginalRate * 0.5 (half-effect over a 2-year alternating cycle)
prerequisiteData: Whether client can prepay next-year charitable gifts in December (cash basis)
Whether client has flexibility on property-tax timing
strategies.action: Stack two years of charitable cash giving into one tax year (e.g. December prepay + January next year). Take the standard deduction in the off-year. Estimated avg annual benefit ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Roth conversion window — fill the bracket
strategies.trigger: federalMarginalRate < 0.24 AND taxpayerAge BETWEEN 30 AND 72 (or unknown)
strategies.formula: conversion = next-bracket headroom; estSavings = conversion * (expectedFutureRate - currentRate). expectedFutureRate placeholder 0.32.
prerequisiteData: Traditional IRA balance
Expected future marginal rate (CPA judgment call)
Whether client expects to itemize in conversion year
strategies.action: Convert ~{{conversion}} of traditional IRA to Roth this year — taxable at the current marginal rate (~{{currentRate}}) but tax-free forever in Roth. Net estimated benefit ~{{estSavings}} assuming a future rate of 32%.

strategies.name: AMT timing — spread ISO exercises or same-year disqualifying sale
strategies.formula: estSavings = amtTax (entirely deferrable by spreading exercises across years OR disqualifying sale in same year)
prerequisiteData: ISO exercise schedule and grant dates
Whether client is willing to do a same-year disqualifying sale (loses LTCG character)
strategies.action: Restructure the ISO exercise plan: spread exercises across tax years to stay below the AMT exemption phase-in, OR execute a same-year disqualifying sale to convert bargain element from AMT preference to ordinary W-2 income. Estimated AMT eliminated ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: NIIT cliff avoidance — get AGI below the 3.8% threshold
strategies.trigger: AGI within +/- $10,000 of NIIT threshold AND netInvestmentIncome > 0
strategies.formula: estSavings = niitTax (currently paid; defer ordinary income or accelerate deductions to drop AGI below threshold)
prerequisiteData: Deferrable income (year-end bonus, RSU vest timing)
Acceleratable deductions (charitable cash, HSA, 401(k) employee deferral)
strategies.action: Drop AGI below the NIIT threshold of {{threshold}} by deferring ~{{deferAmount}} of ordinary income to next year OR accelerating an HSA/401(k)/charitable contribution. Estimated NIIT saved ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: §199A QBI deduction — wage/UBIA limit planning
strategies.trigger: K-1 pass-through income > 0 AND taxable income in §199A phase-in window
strategies.formula: estSavings = qbi * 0.20 * federalMarginalRate * fraction_lost_in_phase_in (proxy 0.50 within band)
prerequisiteData: W-2 wages paid by the pass-through entity (Box 1)
UBIA of qualified property held by the entity
Whether activity is a Specified Service Trade or Business (SSTB)
strategies.action: Within the §199A phase-in band, restructure W-2 wages and/or property holdings inside the entity to qualify for the wage/UBIA-based limit on QBI. Estimated recovered QBI deduction value ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Charitable Donor-Advised Fund (DAF) bunching
strategies.formula: estSavings = (charitableCash * 2) * marginalRate * 0.20 (3-year bunching shifts std-ded cliff by ~20% of stacked total)
prerequisiteData: Whether the client already has a DAF account
Cash-flow capacity for a 2-3 year forward gift
strategies.action: Open a DAF and front-load 2-3 years of charitable cash into one tax year to clear the standard-deduction cliff, then distribute the grants over time. Estimated benefit ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Tax-loss harvesting against $3k ordinary offset
strategies.formula: estSavings = (3000 - capitalLossDeducted) * ordinaryMarginalRate (the $3k ordinary offset; carryforward beyond)
prerequisiteData: Unrealized loss inventory in brokerage account(s)
Whether client trades in a wash-sale-prone way (would defeat the harvest)
strategies.action: Before year-end, realize ~$3,000 of net long-term or short-term capital losses from underperforming positions; reinvest in a non-substantially-identical security. Estimated savings ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Foreign Tax Credit claim vs. deduction
strategies.formula: estSavings = foreign_tax_paid - foreignTaxCredit.credit (direct $-for-$ credit vs Sch A deduction)
prerequisiteData: Country-by-country foreign tax paid (for Form 1116 Schedule B if > $20k)
Foreign-source taxable income for the actual Form 1116 limit
strategies.action: Claim foreign taxes paid as a Foreign Tax Credit on Form 1116 instead of (or in addition to) an itemized deduction. Estimated additional savings ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Persistent NIIT exposure — structural advice
strategies.trigger: niitTax > 0 in current year AND niitTax > 0 in >= 1 prior year
strategies.formula: estSavings = avg(niitTax across years) * 0.5 (structural changes — entity restructure, loss harvesting, deferred-comp shifting — typically recover ~50% of recurring NIIT)
prerequisiteData: Investment income breakdown by source (dividends, interest, capital gains, rental)
Whether client has flexibility on entity structure (S-corp election, real-estate professional status)
Whether client can systematically harvest losses against capital gains each year
strategies.action: NIIT has applied for {{years}} consecutive years averaging ~{{avgNiit}}/year. Consider structural changes — entity restructure, systematic loss harvesting, deferred-comp timing, or moving income-producing assets into qualified retirement accounts. Estimated annual recoverable benefit ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Persistent AMT exposure — preference review
strategies.trigger: amtTax > 0 in current year AND amtTax > 0 in >= 1 prior year
strategies.formula: estSavings = avg(amtTax across years) * 0.4 (AMT preferences — ISO timing, depreciation method, state-tax bunching — typically recover ~40% of recurring AMT)
prerequisiteData: Source of AMT preferences (ISO bargain, depreciation, state tax addback)
Whether client has flexibility on ISO exercise timing in future years
Whether client itemizes (state-tax bunching could shift AMT preference)
strategies.action: AMT has applied for {{years}} consecutive years averaging ~{{avgAmt}}/year. Schedule a Form 6251 review — most recurring AMT comes from ISO timing, depreciation method choice, or state-tax addback. Restructuring preferences across years can recover ~{{estSavings}}/year.

strategies.name: Permanent bunching strategy — persistent std-ded cliff
strategies.trigger: itemizedTotal within +/- 15% of stdDed in current AND >= 1 prior year (persistent cliff) AND charitableCash > 0 in current year
strategies.formula: estSavings = stdDed * 0.25 * marginalRate (single cycle benefit; pattern persistence raises confidence vs G1.3)
prerequisiteData: Whether client has flexibility to prepay 2 years of charitable cash in December
Whether client has flexibility on property-tax timing (acceleration)
Whether client wants to open a DAF account (vs direct charity prepayments)
strategies.action: Itemized deductions have hovered within +/- 15% of the standard-deduction threshold for {{years}} consecutive years. This is a permanent pattern — commit to a 2-year alternating bunching strategy (or open a DAF) to capture ~{{estSavings}}/year on average.

strategies.name: Capital loss carryforward unused — plan gain realization
strategies.trigger: (capitalLossCarryforwardShort + capitalLossCarryforwardLong) > $20,000 in current year AND has not declined materially YoY (delta <= $1k OR grew)
strategies.formula: estSavings = min(currentCarryforward, $20,000) * marginalRate (potential to offset up to $20k of realized capital gains using the carryforward)
prerequisiteData: Whether client has appreciated positions that could be realized
Whether client's investment strategy is consistent with realizing gains (rebalancing, gifting basis-stepped, sale)
Whether the carryforward is short-term or long-term (preferential rate matters)
strategies.action: Capital loss carryforward of ~{{carryforward}} has remained unused for {{years}} years. Plan to realize gains (rebalancing or specific-lot sales) to absorb up to $20k of the carryforward — estimated annual benefit ~{{estSavings}}.

strategies.name: Passive activity loss suspension growing — exit or activate
strategies.trigger: (scheduleEPassiveLossSuspended + k1PassiveLossSuspended) > $5,000 in current year AND has GROWN year-over-year (more suspended this year than last)
strategies.formula: estSavings = (currentSuspended - priorSuspended) * marginalRate * 0.5 (potential to release the year-over-year delta through disposition or qualifying as real-estate-professional)
prerequisiteData: Whether client has any passive INCOME activities to apply against the loss
Whether client would qualify for real-estate-professional status (750 hours, > 50% of personal services)
Whether client is willing to dispose of one passive activity to release that activity's suspended PAL
strategies.action: Passive losses suspended under §469 have grown by ~{{growth}} year-over-year (to ~{{currentSuspended}}). The suspended balance compounds annually without an exit. Consider: (a) full disposition of one activity to fully deduct THAT activity's suspended PAL, (b) qualifying as real-estate professional, or (c) adding a passive-income activity. Estimated annual benefit ~{{estSavings}}.
